skill.name: schema-markup
skill.description: Design, validate, and optimize schema.org structured data for eligibility, correctness, and measurable SEO impact.
skill.summary: Create accurate JSON-LD schema markup that follows Google rich result guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks, a schema.org context URL, and security-like words in SEO documentation. Review found no executable code, no real shell invocation, no network request logic, and no prompt injection attempt. The skill only declares Read, Glob, and Grep tools and provides structured data guidance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Interpreted as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md:267 and SKILL.md:325. These lines are Markdown formatting for schema terms and a JSON example block, not executable commands. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside Markdown documentation and does not define or invoke a script. The skill allowed-tools list does not include shell execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Schema.org URL Interpreted as Network Access
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a hardcoded URL at SKILL.md:327. The URL is the standard schema.org JSON-LD context shown in an example, not a fetch target or exfiltration endpoint. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears as data in a JSON-LD example. No network-capable command or tool is requested by the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SEO Guidance Interpreted as Blocker Patterns
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance patterns across documentation lines. The reviewed lines contain schema markup guidance, scoring bands, validation advice, and SEO policy language. No evidence found of cryptography, reconnaissance, data collection, or malicious intent. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose-only SEO and schema validation guidance at the flagged locations. The file contains no code paths that could perform reconnaissance or cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Schema markup can create SEO risk when it misrepresents page content or ignores Google requirements. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan accurate JSON-LD with validation steps.
actual_capabilities: Scores pages with a Schema Eligibility and Impact Index.
Recommends valid schema types for visible page content.
Flags misleading, incomplete, or unsupported structured data.
Drafts JSON-LD implementation guidance for common page types.
Provides placement and validation checklists for deployment.
Explains when schema should not be implemented.
limitations: Does not guarantee Google rich result display.
Requires accurate page content and business data from the user.
Does not run external validation tools by itself.
Does not replace legal or search engine policy review.

use_cases.title: Audit Existing Structured Data
use_cases.description: Review current schema for accuracy, eligibility, and policy risks before publishing updates.

use_cases.title: Plan Product Page Markup
use_cases.description: Identify required product properties and avoid invalid offers, ratings, or hidden content.

use_cases.title: Scale Template-Based JSON-LD
use_cases.description: Define maintainable schema rules for CMS templates or programmatic landing pages.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic Eligibility
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page content for schema eligibility. Score it using the Schema Eligibility and Impact Index, then tell me whether to implement schema.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Schema Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Given this page type, visible content, and SEO goal, recommend the schema types that are valid. Explain any types I should avoid.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Implementation Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a schema strategy for this page. Include supported types, required properties, placement guidance, and a validation checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Scaled Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this schema plan for a templated site. Identify data sources, maintenance risks, invalid edge cases, and rules for when schema must be suppressed.

output_examples.input: A product page has price and stock status visible, but reviews are stored only in an admin panel.
output_examples.output: The product schema is eligible if visible price, availability, and offer data are accurate. Review or aggregate rating markup should be excluded until ratings are visible to users.

output_examples.input: A blog article needs structured data for search visibility.
output_examples.output: The response recommends article markup, verifies author and date fields, notes image requirements, and provides a validation checklist before release.

output_examples.input: A CMS template creates local business pages for many locations.
output_examples.output: The response defines required location fields, warns against placeholder addresses, and recommends suppressing markup when data is incomplete.

best_practices: Only mark up content that users can see on the page.
Validate schema before deployment and monitor Search Console after release.
Use template rules that suppress markup when required data is missing.
anti_patterns: Adding review markup for ratings that are not visible to users.
Using every possible schema type without a clear search purpose.
Hardcoding stale prices, dates, availability, or business details.

faq.question: Does this skill guarantee rich results?
faq.answer: No. It improves eligibility and correctness, but Google controls whether rich results appear.

faq.question: Can it create JSON-LD for ecommerce pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide product schema when price, availability, offers, and related details are visible and accurate.

faq.question: Does it validate schema automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides validation steps, but users still need Google Rich Results Test or another validator.

faq.question: Can it help remove risky schema?
faq.answer: Yes. It identifies misleading, unsupported, or incomplete markup and explains why removal is safer.

faq.question: Is it useful for CMS templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps define reusable rules and suppression logic for incomplete or invalid records.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The skill declares support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
